rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOSEPHINE STAMPS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. Notice was given to Glenn E. Stamps and Allen J. Stamps only in care of their attorneys, not also to them directly, and this Examiner has been unable to find on the Court's register of actions their written direction to send notice just to their attorneys. [CRC 7.51(a)(2).] 2. The statutory compensation shown on Exhibit 1 to the petition is miscalculated: the total should be $33,462.59, not $33,925.12. 3. It appears that attorney fees paid to the litigation attorneys were all authorized, but the accounting does not show this clearly or indicate the dates orders authorizing such fees. Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 10th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JOHN LAWERENCE WEST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) It appears either the petition needs to be amended to distribute to the domiciliary probate or probates needs to be opened in California and the petition amended to distribute to the personal representatives for both Anna Louise Ashley and Jeanette Turner because the distribution cannot be made by 13100 due to the gross fair market of both Anna and Jeanette's estate exceeding $166,250.00. 2) The petition does not provide good cause for the account exceeding 2 years. Unless otherwise permitted for good cause, if an account exceeds two years, the first two years must be filed separately. After the first two-year account is adjudicated, subsequent account periods may not be for more than three (3) years and each three-year period must be filed separately. If all interested parties waive an account by the personal representative, the waiver applies to the entire estate administration, not just to a periodic report on the status of administration. [See LRSF 14.34 A.] 3) The filing fee for the petition for probate was $450.00. Please explain why $500.00 is sought as reimbursement. 4) Petitioner is requesting $9.00 in reimbursement for parking. Parking is absorbed as part of fees and the court will not allow reimbursement for parking. [See LRSF 14.57 B.] 5) The account ending date is 11/17/2021. Petitioner must provide statements that verify the balance at the end of the account period on 11/17/2021. The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. Ending balances shown in the account must be reconciled to the statements or letters, which must be filed. Unless filed confidentially in a guardianship or conservatorship case, account numbers should be redacted as set forth in CRC 1.201. [LRSF 14.34 D.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 03/14/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing. close of estate set for 3/28/23.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE TRUST CREATED UNDER THE WILL OF KATHERINE HENDERSON, DECEASED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Accepting Resignation Of Trustee And For Appointment Of Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing. order revised to make appointment of new trustee effective on the date the resignation of the current trustee is effective and to strike #2 as duplicative and the court was not asked to approve trustee and attorney fees.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE AGNES CLAIRE KOZEL REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED MARCH 7, 1991
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 4/6/22. No courtesy copies. No proposed order. [LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7 A. and B., ad 14.59 E.] The correct form of notice for a Probate Code Sect. 850 petition is JC form DE-115. All courtesy copies and a proposed order must be RECEIVED by the assigned Examiner by no later than 3/25/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE YANG SU CHO 2015 LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Beneficiary'S Petition To Remove The Trustee And Order Instructions Pursuant To Probate Code 17200
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 04/11/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Must give 30 day's notice on the mandatory judicial council form DE-120. 2) Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. 3) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the envelope or a cover page, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/28/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF PHEROZE JOSHI
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: grant without hearing. close of estate set for 2/28/23.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF KATHLEEN MARY FENNONE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. Continued again for same issues that were emailed to attorney 01/12/2022. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 3/14/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.] Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JUAN M. LANZARIN
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: continued to 3/28/22 for petitioner to address the following issues: new publication is required for the new hearing date, as the first publication was only 14 days before the date of the initial hearing, when 15 days is required by probate code section 8121 [please note, this issue is not cured by the hearing date having been continued]; there is no confidential attachment to the duties and liabilities form on file [cannot be efiled and must have a caption page]; a supplement must be filed [signed and verified] that states the relationship of the persons listed in #8 of the petition to the decedent [listing as devisee and executor is not sufficient as those are not relationships to the decedent]. filing deadline is noon on 3/18/22. please note, this petition will require an appearance as petitioner seeks appointmennt in pro per with no bond.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHRISTOPHER GLENN GARRITY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 03/30/2022 per counsel's request (via e-mail on 02/15/2022). Notes were e-mailed to counsel on 02/16/2022, even though no courtesy copy of the petition and proposed order had been received.
